already_told_agent: s01_signups_dropped: I don't do SQL or write code, just look at the CSV I exported.
s01_signups_dropped: Give me a list of possible causes, most likely first, not a whole essay.
s02_welcome_emails: Loops has a direct Supabase integration so Zapier is not needed.
s02_welcome_emails: Use the Loops + Supabase integration to automatically send the welcome email when a user signs up.
s02_welcome_emails: The process should take Andrew 15–20 minutes, maybe less, with no code or Zapier required.
s03_aws_bill: I don’t want to deal with any CLI right now, only use the S3 console.
s04_launch_image: I don’t use Midjourney — I have DALL-E through ChatGPT and Canva.
s04_launch_image: Give me a version that’ll really make the navy stand out, almost like #172455 from our brand guide.
s04_launch_image: I want the “Updates that don’t need a meeting” tagline to pop and be super legible, like bright white font on navy if needed.
s04_launch_image: Spell out the actual color hex # for navy (#172455) in the prompt, not just “deep navy”.
s04_launch_image: Only use “Accent Blue” (#60A5FA) and “Success Green” (#22C55E) from the brand guide for highlights, not orange or teal.
s04_launch_image: The image should be unmistakably Teamflow and extremely on-brand.
s04_launch_image: No people, zero orange or teal, landscape 16:9 format.
s05_investor_update: Make the investor update sound more conversational and like me, not like a press release.
s05_investor_update: Make the update much more bullet-point heavy.
s05_investor_update: Use 'Marketing' and 'Feature launches' as section headers instead of 'LinkedIn Ads' and 'Product'.
s05_investor_update: For the 'Ask' section, pick one example and drop it in so the email is ready to send, not left as a generic placeholder.
s05_investor_update: This is an email, not a deck—keep everything as brief as possible.
s06_status_page: I really don’t want to manage another server or deal with custom code.
s07_test_accounts: I don't run SQL myself—Andrew does.
s07_test_accounts: Write instructions as a short Slack message I can paste to Andrew.
s07_test_accounts: Andrew needs to be extra cautious in Supabase Studio—a wrong query or missing filter could zap real accounts.
s07_test_accounts: Always get the dry-run CSV first, double-check every flagged account, and only delete after I give the OK.
s07_test_accounts: Never skip the review step before deleting accounts.
s07_test_accounts: Andrew should do a dry-run with a SELECT query first, export the flagged accounts to CSV, and wait for my approval before doing anything destructive.
s07_test_accounts: Always double-check the list to avoid losing real accounts.
s07_test_accounts: Draft backup instructions for Andrew before deleting anything.
s07_test_accounts: Ask Andrew to confirm if deleting users in Supabase will update Mixpanel, or if manual cleanup is needed.
s08_stripe_failures: 3DS just means 'extra card security'—banks ask customers for a code or approval to stop fraud.
s08_stripe_failures: Stripe usually handles the 3DS step automatically, so Teamflow shouldn’t need to change anything.
s08_stripe_failures: If your setup doesn’t force those checks, sometimes payments get declined instead.
s08_stripe_failures: Andrew should double-check Stripe settings on Monday to make sure 3DS is required for payments that need it.
s08_stripe_failures: For now, the only thing you can do is ask customers to try again, or use a different card.
s08_stripe_failures: Unless you see a Stripe option to turn on more 'authentication' in the dashboard, it’s a wait game.
s08_stripe_failures: Andrew hates reading essays—keep Slack messages to him short.
s08_stripe_failures: The Stripe dashboard settings for 3DS and Radar are quick and easy to find: Settings > Payment methods > 3D Secure & Authentication, and Settings > Fraud prevention.
s08_stripe_failures: Add a note in the Slack message for Andrew to double-check if Stripe auto-handles authentication.
s09_ga4_goals: If instructions have more than 7 steps, break them up or make them simplermy brain maxes out around step 5.
s10_holdout_board_meeting: Give me a slide-by-slide outline with a single bullet per slide, no paragraphs.
s10_holdout_board_meeting: The most important slide is the Metrics overview (MRR, growth rate, retention); that's what investors care about most.
